on tenex-edge tmux allow me to see past sessions from the past x hours and allow me to easily change the number of hours of the filter, default it to 4 hours
Implemented time-filtered exited sessions panel with [e] toggle and [+]/[-] hour adjustment, defaulting to 4 hours.

perform an analysis of what happened in this session thus far -- this session didn't publish anything as a title for the session! that's like a fucking crime! why didn't this session trigger publishing a title that would show up in `tenex-edge tmux`'s list?

Root-caused two bugs: distillation had no timeout so a slow API call froze the entire engine for 6+ minutes, and last_distill was set unconditionally even on failure, consuming the one retry slot per turn.

1. the moment the user sends the message, if we don't have a title, it should be set to the user's message (titleized, truncated).
2. time-limit the distillation call, 20s max, make it async so it doesn't lock anyhting -- retry a few times if it times out
3. fix the second bug so that a failure doesn't set it as if it had been distilled

Implemented all three fixes: immediate title seeding from user prompt at rising edge, async background distillation with 20s timeout via JoinHandle, and last_distill only set on success with separate attempt tracking for retries.
Committed engine fixes (src/runtime.rs, src/transcript.rs) as 2ef86f4b; tmux_cli.rs had already been committed by an external ratatui migration.
